audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for famine journey, Joseph's recognition, spy accusation, three-day custody, Simeon, money in sacks, and Jacob's refusal over Benjamin.

verses.restored: Jacob saw that there was grain in Egypt and said to his sons, Why do you look at one another?
He said, Look, I have heard there is grain in Egypt. Go down there and buy for us, so that we may live and not die.
Ten of Joseph’s brothers went down to buy grain from Egypt.
But Benjamin, Joseph’s brother, Jacob did not send with his brothers, for he said, Lest harm meet him.
The sons of Israel came to buy among those who came, for the famine was in the land of Canaan.
Joseph was the ruler over the land; he was the one selling to all the people of the earth. Joseph’s brothers came and bowed to him with faces to the ground.
Joseph saw his brothers and recognized them, but he made himself a stranger to them and spoke harshly, saying, Where have you come from? They said, From the land of Canaan to buy food.
Joseph recognized his brothers, but they did not recognize him.
Joseph remembered the dreams he had dreamed about them and said, You are spies; you have come to see the nakedness of the land.
They said to him, No, my lord; your servants have come to buy food.
We are all sons of one man; we are honest. Your servants are not spies.
He said to them, No, you have come to see the nakedness of the land.
They said, Your servants are twelve brothers, sons of one man in the land of Canaan. Look, the youngest is with our father today, and one is no more.
Joseph said to them, It is as I spoke to you: you are spies.
By this you shall be tested: by the life of Pharaoh, you shall not go out from here unless your youngest brother comes here.
Send one of you to get your brother, while you remain bound, so your words may be tested, whether truth is with you; if not, by the life of Pharaoh, surely you are spies.
He gathered them into custody three days.
On the third day Joseph said to them, Do this and live, for I fear the Divine.
If you are honest, let one of your brothers be bound in your prison house; you go and bring grain for the famine of your houses.
Bring your youngest brother to me, so your words will be verified and you will not die. And they did so.
They said to one another, Truly we are guilty concerning our brother, because we saw the distress of his soul when he pleaded with us, and we did not listen. Therefore this distress has come upon us.
Reuben answered, Did I not tell you, Do not sin against the boy? But you did not listen. Now his blood is being required.
They did not know that Joseph understood, for the interpreter was between them.
He turned away from them and wept. Then he returned and spoke to them, took Simeon from them, and bound him before their eyes.
Joseph commanded that their vessels be filled with grain, that each man’s silver be returned to his sack, and that provisions be given for the way; and it was done for them.
They loaded their grain on their donkeys and went from there.
One opened his sack to give his donkey feed at the lodging place, and he saw his silver, and look, it was in the mouth of his sack.
He said to his brothers, My silver has been returned, and look, it is in my sack. Their hearts went out, and they trembled, saying, What is this the Divine has done to us?
They came to Jacob their father in the land of Canaan and told him all that had happened to them.
The man, lord of the land, spoke harshly with us and treated us as spies of the land.
We said to him, We are honest; we are not spies.
We are twelve brothers, sons of our father; one is no more, and the youngest is today with our father in Canaan.
The man, lord of the land, said to us, By this I will know that you are honest: leave one brother with me, take grain for the famine of your houses, and go.
Bring your youngest brother to me; then I will know you are not spies but honest men. I will give your brother to you, and you may trade in the land.
As they emptied their sacks, look, each man’s bundle of silver was in his sack. When they and their father saw the bundles of silver, they were afraid.
Jacob their father said, You have bereaved me: Joseph is no more, Simeon is no more, and Benjamin you would take. All this is upon me.
Reuben spoke to his father, saying, You may put my two sons to death if I do not bring him back to you. Put him in my hand, and I will return him to you.
He said, My son shall not go down with you, for his brother is dead and he alone remains. If harm meets him on the way you go, you will bring down my gray hair in sorrow to Sheol.
